zh.lifeSummary: 當Annabella Smith alias Bland於1613年出生在英國艾可可爾斯特時，她的父親John Bland41歲，母親Isabella Drake35歲。她於大約1634年與William Barsham在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可沃特托結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1683年8月23日之前在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可沃特托，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州米德萊可沃特托笛卡爾 波英 普拉斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Annabella Smith alias Bland于1613年出生在英国艾可可爾斯特时，她的父亲John Bland41岁，母亲Isabella Drake35岁。她于大约1634年与William Barsham在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可沃特托结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1683年8月23日之前在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可沃特托，埋葬在美国马萨诸塞州米德萊可沃特托笛卡爾 波英 普拉斯。

nameMeanings.name: Smith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

variants.name: Smithe
meanings: English: variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: Smither
meanings: English (Surrey): occupational name from Middle English smith(i)ere, smeth(i)ere ‘blacksmith’, a derivative of Middle English smithen, smethen, ‘to make (something) in metal, to forge’. Compare Smith .

variants.name: Smithey
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from Middle English smithi ‘smithy, forge’ (Old Norse smithja), used either as a topographic name for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, or an occupational name for someone who worked in one. Compare Smith and Smead .

variants.name: Smyth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ronan, Aidan, Brigid, Conor, Eoin, Malachy, Paddy.
English: variant of   Smith  . This form of the surname is particularly common Ireland.

variants.name: Smythe
meanings: English (London): variant of   Smith  .

variants.name: McGowan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Siobhan, Cormac, Seamus, Aileen, Colm, Eithne, Kieran, Niall, Paddy.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (a') Ghobhainn or Mac Gobhann (Scottish) or Mac (an) Ghabhann (Irish), ‘son of (the) smith’. This name has also been Anglicized as Smith and Smithson .

variants.name: Smead
meanings: English:
occupational name from Middle English smeth(e) ‘smith’, a phonetic variant of Smith 1 that was widespread in medieval England, especially in south-eastern and south-western counties.

topographic, occupational, and habitational name from Middle English smethe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smethe), a side form of Smith 2, for someone who lived and worked at a blacksmith's, or who lived in or came from a place so named, such as Smeeth (Kent) or Smeed Farm in Hastingleigh (Kent). Compare the synonymous Smithey .

variants.name: Faber
meanings: German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Danish; Slovak and Czech (mainly Fáber): occupational name from Latin faber ‘blacksmith’. At the time of the Reformation, it was much used as a humanistic name, a translation into Latin of vernacular surnames such as German Schmidt and Dutch Smit . Compare Fabri and Fauber .

History: In North America, this surname was originally also part of a title. It was François Lefebvre, sieur Duplessis Faber from Paris, France, whose title sieur Duplessis Faber was shortened into the surname Faber. He married Marie-Madeleine Chorel in Champlain, QC, in 1689.

nameMeanings.name: alias
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Juan, Manuel, Jorge, Jesus, Arturo, Ramon, Pedro, Francisco, Miguel.
Spanish: from the medieval personal name Arias, of disputed origin.

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Spanish surname (see above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

nameMeanings.name: Bland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place called Bland in Sedburgh, Yorkshire, the origin of which is uncertain. Possibly it is from Old English (ge)bland ‘storm, commotion’ (from blandan ‘to blend or mingle’), with reference to its exposed situation. The modern English adjective bland did not come into English (from Latin) until the 15th century, and is therefore unlikely to have given rise to surnames.
French: nickname from Old French blant ‘flattering’ (from Latin blandus).

variants.name: Blandin
meanings: French: derivative of   Bland  .
Altered form of   Blanding  , a surname of English origin.

variants.name: Blend
meanings: Possibly English: variant of   Bland  .
German: unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: Annabella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Sometimes taken as an elaboration of   Anna  , but more probably a dissimilated form of   Amabel .  It has been common in Scotland since the 12th century and was still in use in England in the 1600s. Its recent revival in popularity in England and elsewhere dates from the 1940s.

variants.name: Annabell
meanings: Sometimes taken as an elaboration of   Anna  , but more probably a dissimilated form of   Amabel .  It has been common in Scotland since the 12th century and was still in use in England in the 1600s. Its recent revival in popularity in England and elsewhere dates from the 1940s.

variants.name: Annabelle
meanings: Sometimes taken as an elaboration of   Anna  , but more probably a dissimilated form of   Amabel .  It has been common in Scotland since the 12th century and was still in use in England in the 1600s. Its recent revival in popularity in England and elsewhere dates from the 1940s.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Annabella Smith alias Bland was born in 1613, in Colchester, Essex, England, her father, John Bland, was 41 and her mother, Isabella Drake, was 35. She married William Barsham about 1634, in Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. She died before 23 August 1683, in Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, and was buried in Old Burying Place, Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Annabella Smith alias Bland est née en 1613, à Colchester, Essex, Angleterre, son père, John Bland, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Isabella Drake, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé William Barsham environ 1634, à Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée avant 23 août 1683, à Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, et a été enterrée à Old Burying Place, Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Annabella Smith alias Bland 1613 in Colchester, Essex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Bland 41 und ihre Mutter Isabella Drake 35. Sie hat um 1634 in Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America William Barsham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb vor 23. August 1683 in Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Old Burying Place, Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Annabella Smith alias Bland è nata il nel 1613 nel Colchester, Essex, England, sua padre, John Bland, aveva 41 anni e sua madre, Isabella Drake, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato William Barsham circa 1634, nel Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie. Morì prima del 23 agosto 1683, nel Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, e fu sepolta a Old Burying Place, Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.Old Burying Place, Watertown, Middlesex,: アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ミッドレセックスワテルトウンオルド ブリイング プレイス
ja.Watertown, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Bri: イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーワテルトウン
ja.Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay: イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスワテルトウン
ja.Colchester, Essex, England: イギリスエセックスコルチェスター
ja.lifeSummary: Annabella Smith alias Blandが1613年にイギリスエセックスコルチェスターで生まれた時，父John Blandは41歳，母Isabella Drakeは35歳であった。約1634年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスワテルトウンでWilliam Barshamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1683年8月23日以前にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスワテルトウンで死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ミッドレセックスワテルトウンオルド ブリイング プレイスにて埋葬された。

ko.Martha's Vineyard, West Tisbury, Dukes,: 미국 매사추세츠 주 드크스 웨스트 티스버이 마스아'스 빈야어드
ko.Old Burying Place, Watertown, Middlesex,: 미국 매사추세츠 주 미들레세스 왓르타운 드 버이잉 플레이스
ko.Watertown, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Bri: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 왓르타운
ko.Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 왓르타운
ko.lifeSummary: Annabella Smith alias Bland(이)가 1613년 영국 에세스 코체스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Bland(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella Drake(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 대략 1634년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 왓르타운에서 William Barsham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1683년 8월 23일 이전 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 왓르타운에서 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 미들레세스 왓르타운 드 버이잉 플레이스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Annabella Smith alias Bland nasceu em 1613, em Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Bland, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Isabella Drake, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com William Barsham aproximadamente 1634, em Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 23 de agosto de 1683, em Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, e foi sepultada em Old Burying Place, Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Annabella Smith alias Bland родилась в 1613 г. в местности колчестир, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени John Bland было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Isabella Drake, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Barsham около 1634 г. в местности вэитиртоун, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла перед 23 августа 1683 г. в местности вэитиртоун, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика и была похоронена в местности олд буринг плеис, вэитиртоун, миддлесекс, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Annabella Smith alias Bland nació en 1613, en Colchester, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, John Bland, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Isabella Drake, tenía 35 años. Se casó con William Barsham aproximadamente 1634, en Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió antes de 23 de agosto de 1683, en Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, y fue sepultada en Old Burying Place, Watertown, Middlesex, Masachusets, Estados Unidos.
